title: group-add=keep-groups not leaking supplementary group
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I am not able to leak my supplementary groups into a rootless container. I can preserve the primary group and user id, but not the secondary groups.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
$ id
uid=1000(foo) gid=1001(foo) groups=1001(foo),495(graphite)
$ podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add=keep-groups --runtime=/usr/bin/crun alpine id
uid=1000(foo) gid=1001(foo) groups=65534(nobody),1001(foo)
$ podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add=keep-groups --runtime=/usr/bin/crun --annotation run.oci.keep_original_groups=1 alpine id
uid=1000(foo) gid=1001(foo) groups=65534(nobody),1001(foo)

**Describe the results you received:**
Group "graphite" from outside the container is mapped to "nobody" inside the container.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Group "graphite" leaking into the container, so it would appear as "graphite", and I would be able to access files outside of the container for a mounted volume.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
The full command I'm trying to use is
```
$ podman run  --volume=/storage/ezb_test:/ezb --storage-driver overlay --storage-opt "overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs" --userns=keep-id --runtime=/usr/bin/crun -it registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi ls -al /ezb
total 0
drwxrwxr-x  2 nobody nobody 4096 Jun 30 17:08 .
dr-xr-xr-x 19 root   root     80 Jun 30 18:34 ..
-rwxrwxrwx  1 nobody nobody    0 Jun 30 17:07 admins-2e
-rwxrwxrwx  1 nobody nobody    0 Jun 17 20:34 ezb5568
-rwxrwxrwx  1 foo    nobody    0 Jun 30 17:08 graphite
-rwxrwxrwx  1 nobody nobody    0 Jun 27 21:33 group_swst
-rwxrwxrwx  1 nobody nobody    0 Jun 17 20:34 root
```
The group on the file graphite is supposed to be "graphite", for example. I've also tried adding --annotation run.oci.keep_original_groups=1 to that, but I think that option is obviated now by using group-add=keep-groups (along with using crun)?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.0.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body:
Are you sure groups combines the two NOBODY together.

Try running sleep inside of a container and then look at /proc/$PID/status of the container where $PID is a process inside of the container, and see if the groups are there.

I stated my question poorly. Outside the container
```
-rwxrwxrwx 1 bin     admins-2e 0 Jun 30 13:07 admins-2e
-rwxrwxrwx 1 ezb5568 ezb5568   0 Jun 17 16:34 ezb5568
-rwxrwxrwx 1 foo     graphite  0 Jun 30 13:08 graphite
-rwxrwxrwx 1 nobody  swst      0 Jun 27 17:33 group_swst
-rwxrwxrwx 1 root    root      0 Jun 17 16:34 root
```
Inside the container
```
-rwxrwxrwx  1 nobody  nobody     0 Jun 30 17:07 admins-2e
-rwxrwxrwx  1 ezb5568 ezb5568    0 Jun 17 20:34 ezb5568
-rwxrwxrwx  1 nobody  nobody     0 Jun 30 17:08 graphite
-rwxrwxrwx  1 nobody  nobody     0 Jun 27 21:33 group_swst
-rwxrwxrwx  1 nobody  nobody     0 Jun 17 20:34 root
```
Inside should show the group on file group_swst as "swst" and the group on file graphite as "graphite". --userns=keep-id leaks the primary group, but I'm not getting the secondary groups.

```
$ id
uid=1000(foo) gid=1001(foo) groups=1001(foo),495(graphite)
```

The secondary groups are not mapped inside the user namespace.  So when you are inside the user namespace, these groups do not exist and the kernel maps them to the overflow gid (by default the nobody user).

If you look from the outside then they exist because they are known in the host environment.

The kernel will use them to validate access to the file system.

Right, but I'm using
```
podman run --userns=keep-id --group-add=keep-groups --runtime=/usr/bin/crun alpine id
```
Shouldn't that leak the secondary groups into the container?

it does.

But you cannot see them when you are in the user namespace, because these GIDs do not exist there.  You need to inspect the process from the host.  The easiest way is to look at the `Groups` field in the `/proc/$CONTAINER_PID/status` file
We need to fix podman top $CID HGROUP
could we just print a warning when the container has supplementary groups?
